revision.text:
[[Category:dd]][[ja:車折神社]]
{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Shrine |name = Kurumazaki Shrine |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecrated = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine suddenly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] - '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kurumazaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Tenryu-ji sub-temples]] [[Category:Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Kiyohara clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Sagano and Arashiyama]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]]

== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] - '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kurumazaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Tenryu-ji sub-temples]] [[Category:Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Kiyohara clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Sagano and Arashiyama]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]]

revision.text:
[[Category:dd]][[ja:車折神社]]
{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Shrine |name = Kurumazaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecration_year = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine suddenly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] - '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kurumazaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Tenryu-ji sub-temples]] [[Category:Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Kiyohara clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Sagano and Arashiyama]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]]

== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] - '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.

[[Category:dd]][[ja:車折神社]]
{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Shrine |name = Kurumazaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecration_year = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine suddenly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] - '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kurumazaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Tenryu-ji sub-temples]] [[Category:Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Kiyohara clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Sagano and Arashiyama]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]]

'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine suddenly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] - '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kurumazaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Tenryu-ji sub-temples]] [[Category:Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Kiyohara clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Sagano and Arashiyama]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:kk]]

revision.text:
[[Category:dd]][[ja:車折神社]]
{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Shrine |name = Kurumazaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecration_year = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine suddenly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] - '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kurumazaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Tenryu-ji sub-temples]] [[Category:Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Kiyohara clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Sagano and Arashiyama]] [[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kurumazaki Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:kk]]

== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] - '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.

[[Category:dd]][[ja:車折神社]]
{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Shrine |name = Kurumazaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecration_year = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine suddenly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] - '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kurumazaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Tenryu-ji sub-temples]] [[Category:Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Kiyohara clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Sagano and Arashiyama]] [[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kurumazaki Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]]

'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine suddenly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] - '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kurumazaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Tenryu-ji sub-temples]] [[Category:Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Kiyohara clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Sagano and Arashiyama]] [[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kurumazaki Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]]

== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] - '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kurumazaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Tenryu-ji sub-temples]] [[Category:Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Kiyohara clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Sagano and Arashiyama]] [[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]

revision.text:
[[Category:dd]][[ja:車折神社]]
{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kurumazaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecration_year = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine suddenly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] - '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kurumazaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Tenryu-ji sub-temples]] [[Category:Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Kiyohara clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Sagano and Arashiyama]] [[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]

The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine suddenly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] - '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.

[[Category:dd]][[ja:車折神社]]
{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kurumazaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecration_year = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine suddenly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] - '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.

[[Category:dd]][[ja:車折神社]]
{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kurumazaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecration_year = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine suddenly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] - '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}} {{citation needed|Kurumazaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Tenryu-ji sub-temples]] [[Category:Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Kiyohara clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Sagano and Arashiyama]] [[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]] <!--Kurumazaki Shrine--> [[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qqqq]]
[[Category:Removing this excess]]

== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buqqhism}} {{citation needed|Kurumazaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Tenryu-ji sub-temples]] [[Category:Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buqqhism]] [[Category:Kiyohara clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Sagano and Arashiyama]] [[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]] <!--Kurumazaki Shrine--> [[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
[[Category:qq]][[ja:車折神社]]
{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kurumazaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecration_year = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buqqhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine suqqenly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buqqhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buqqhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] - '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buqqhism}} {{citation needed|Kurumazaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Tenryu-ji sub-temples]] [[Category:Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buqqhism]] [[Category:Kiyohara clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Sagano and Arashiyama]] [[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]] <!--Kurumazaki Shrine--> [[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kurumazaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]]
