revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kurumazaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecration_year = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine su11enly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the Rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] * '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.

== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}} {{citation needed|Kurumazaki Shrine}}

revision.comment: 59 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:車折神社 up to rev 104355782
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|京都市右京区にある車折神社|岐阜県各務原市にある車折神社|車折神社 (各務原市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 車折神社 |画像 = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|車折神社本殿]] |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]嵯峨朝日町23 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 00|緯度秒 = 57 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 41|経度秒 = 21 |祭神 = [[清原頼業]]（[[車折明神]]） |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[文治]]5年（[[1189年]]） |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = 宝寿院（寶壽院）
車折大明神 |札所等 = [[神仏霊場巡拝の道]]第91番（京都第11番） |例祭 = [[5月14日]] |神事 = [[三船祭]] |地図 = Japan Kyoto city }} '''車折神社'''（くるまざきじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[右京区]][[嵯峨野|嵯峨]]朝日町にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。現在は[[神社本庁]]に属さない[[単立]]神社である。氏子を持たず、全国の崇敬者によって成り立っている。「車柝神社」とも表記される{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}。 == 祭神 == 祭神は[[清原頼業]]（きよはらのよりなり）である。頼業は、学問で有名な[[清原広澄|広澄流]][[清原氏 (広澄流)|清原氏]]の出であり、自身も[[平安時代]]後期の著名な[[漢学者]]・[[儒学者]]であった。大外記の職を長年務め、晩年には[[九条兼実]]より政治の諮問を受け、兼実から「その才、神といふべく尊ぶべし」と評された。 == 歴史 == [[文治]]5年（[[1189年]]）に[[清原頼業]]が亡くなると、[[清原氏|清原家]]の領地であった現在地に頼業の廟が設けられ、後に頼業の[[院号]]にちなんで宝寿院という寺院が建立された。その後、宝寿院は清原家の菩提寺となり、その境内に頼業を祀る社が建てられた、それが当社の起源である。なお、頼業は良く[[サクラ|桜]]を愛でていたことから当地には多くの桜が植えられ、別名「桜の宮」と呼ばれていた。 社名の「車折」については、ある人が[[牛車]]に乗ったまま社前を通った所、突然車が裂けてしまったためとも、[[後嵯峨天皇]]の[[桂川 (淀川水系)|大堰川]]遊幸の際、社前で突然車が前に進まなくなったので不思議に思って社の者に問うた所、頼業公を祀ると答えがあったので、還御の後に「車折大明神」の[[神号]]と[[正一位]]の[[神階]]を贈ったためともいう。 [[室町時代]]に[[天龍寺]]が建立されると宝寿院はその末寺となった。 頼業を祀る社が車折神社と呼ばれて一般に信仰が広まるのは[[江戸時代]]以降のようである。 当社は[[明治]]時代になると[[神仏分離]]によって宝寿寺から独立したが、荒廃してしまった。そこに、文人画家で儒学者でもある[[富岡鉄斎]]が宮司として入り、復興させた。 [[近代社格制度]]で[[村社]]に列せられている。 == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[宝暦]]2年（[[1752年]]）再建。[[2014年]]（[[平成]]26年）10月に改修。 * 拝殿 - [[1988年]]（[[昭和]]63年）再建。天井画は[[文化勲章]]を受章した画家・[[山口華楊]]の実兄である[[山口玲熙]]（やまぐちれいき）によるもの。 * 社務所 * 参集殿 * [[富岡鉄斎]]の筆塚 - 当社の元宮司で近代日本画の巨匠・富岡鉄斎が生前に用いた筆2,000本以上が納められている。 * 神門 * 社号柱 - 社号は富岡鉄斎の筆。 == 境内社 == * 芸能神社（げいのうじんじゃ） - 祭神：[[アメノウズメ|天宇受売命]]。[[1957年]]（昭和32年）創建。[[芸能]]・[[芸術]]の分野で活動する人々より崇敬を受けている。芸能人やその関係者によって奉納された約4,000枚もの朱塗りの玉垣が社殿を囲む。 * 八百万神社 - 祭神：八百万の神々 * [[稲荷神社]] - 祭神：[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂神]]。辰巳稲荷神社とも呼ばれる。 * 水神社 - 祭神：龍神 * 清めの社 * [[清少納言]]社 - 祭神：清少納言 * 弁天神社（滄海神社） - 祭神：[[弁財天]]（[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]） * [[大国主]]神社 - 祭神：大国主大神（[[大黒天]]） Kurumazaki Shrine140517NI1.JPG|表参道 Kurumazaki-jinja, ura-sandō.jpg|裏参道 Kurumazaki Shrine140517NI4.JPG|四の鳥居とかつての中門 Kurumazaki-jinja, 2-no-torii 01.jpg|以前の二の鳥居 Kurumazaki Shrine140517NI5.JPG|拝殿 Kurumazaki Shrine140517NI6.JPG|500円で販売されている祈念神石 ファイル:Ōkuninushi-jinja inKurumazaki-jinja, Kyōto.jpg|大国主神社 ファイル:Kurumazaki Shrine140517NI3.JPG|芸能神社 ファイル:折獄龜鑑補(五).djvu|折獄龜鑑補 == 祭事 == [[例祭]]は5月14日で、その延長神事として5月第3日曜日に「三船祭」が行われる。[[昭和天皇]]即位を記念して1928年（昭和3年）より催行されているもので、[[嵐山]]の[[桂川 (淀川水系)|大堰川]]にて平安時代の船遊びが再現される。 == 文化財 == === 重要美術品 === * [[三略]] - [[清原頼業]]の筆によると伝えられる「三略」が[[重要美術品]]に認定されている。 === その他 === 画家・[[富岡鉄斎]]が一時当社の[[宮司]]を務めていた関係から、鉄斎の作品が多数伝わっており、「'''車軒文庫'''」として管理されている。また、社名標や本殿の扁額は鉄斎の筆によるものである。 == 時代劇ロケ == 車折神社は、東映や松竹の撮影所が近くにあることから、映画やドラマのロケによく使用されている。以下は、車折神社で撮影を行った主な時代劇のタイトル。 * [[暴れん坊将軍|吉宗評判記 暴れん坊将軍]] * [[松平右近事件帳]] * [[長七郎江戸日記]] * [[名奉行 遠山の金さん]] * [[半七捕物帳]] * [[忠臣蔵]] * [[はんなり菊太郎]] * [[水戸黄門]] * [[徳川風雲録]] * [[密命 寒月霞斬り]] == 前後の札所 == ; [[神仏霊場巡拝の道]] : 90 [[神護寺]]　-　'''91 車折神社'''　-　92 [[仁和寺]] == 交通 == * [[京福電気鉄道|京福電鉄]][[京福電気鉄道嵐山本線|嵐山本線]]（嵐電）「[[車折神社駅]]」下車すぐ南側 * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]]（[[山陰本線]]）「[[嵯峨嵐山駅]]」下車 徒歩約10分 * [[阪急電鉄]][[阪急嵐山線|嵐山線]]「[[嵐山駅 (阪急)|嵐山駅]]」下車 徒歩約15分 * [[京都市営バス]]「車折神社前」下車 徒歩3分 * [[京都バス]]「車折神社前（嵯峨美術大学・[[嵯峨美術短期大学]]前）」下車 徒歩約3分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp 車折神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{pref-stub|pref=京都府}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くるまさきしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:右京区の歴史]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:臨済宗天龍寺派の寺院]] [[Category:神仏霊場巡拝の道]] [[Category:清原氏 (広澄流)|社くるまさきしんしや]] [[Category:右京区の建築物]] [[Category:嵯峨野・嵐山]]

{{Otheruses|京都市右京区にある車折神社|岐阜県各務原市にある車折神社|車折神社 (各務原市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 車折神社 |画像 = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|車折神社本殿]] |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]嵯峨朝日町23 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 00|緯度秒 = 57 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 41|経度秒 = 21 |祭神 = [[清原頼業]]（[[車折明神]]） |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[文治]]5年（[[1189年]]） |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = 宝寿院（寶壽院）
車折大明神 |札所等 = [[神仏霊場巡拝の道]]第91番（京都第11番） |例祭 = [[5月14日]] |神事 = [[三船祭]] |地図 = Japan Kyoto city }} '''車折神社'''（くるまざきじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[右京区]][[嵯峨野|嵯峨]]朝日町にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。現在は[[神社本庁]]に属さない[[単立]]神社である。氏子を持たず、全国の崇敬者によって成り立っている。「車柝神社」とも表記される{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}。 == 祭神 == 祭神は[[清原頼業]]（きよはらのよりなり）である。頼業は、学問で有名な[[清原広澄|広澄流]][[清原氏 (広澄流)|清原氏]]の出であり、自身も[[平安時代]]後期の著名な[[漢学者]]・[[儒学者]]であった。大外記の職を長年務め、晩年には[[九条兼実]]より政治の諮問を受け、兼実から「その才、神といふべく尊ぶべし」と評された。 == 歴史 == [[文治]]5年（[[1189年]]）に[[清原頼業]]が亡くなると、[[清原氏|清原家]]の領地であった現在地に頼業の廟が設けられ、後に頼業の[[院号]]にちなんで宝寿院という寺院が建立された。その後、宝寿院は清原家の菩提寺となり、その境内に頼業を祀る社が建てられた、それが当社の起源である。なお、頼業は良く[[サクラ|桜]]を愛でていたことから当地には多くの桜が植えられ、別名「桜の宮」と呼ばれていた。 社名の「車折」については、ある人が[[牛車]]に乗ったまま社前を通った所、突然車が裂けてしまったためとも、[[後嵯峨天皇]]の[[桂川 (淀川水系)|大堰川]]遊幸の際、社前で突然車が前に進まなくなったので不思議に思って社の者に問うた所、頼業公を祀ると答えがあったので、還御の後に「車折大明神」の[[神号]]と[[正一位]]の[[神階]]を贈ったためともいう。 [[室町時代]]に[[天龍寺]]が建立されると宝寿院はその末寺となった。 頼業を祀る社が車折神社と呼ばれて一般に信仰が広まるのは[[江戸時代]]以降のようである。 当社は[[明治]]時代になると[[神仏分離]]によって宝寿寺から独立したが、荒廃してしまった。そこに、文人画家で儒学者でもある[[富岡鉄斎]]が宮司として入り、復興させた。 [[近代社格制度]]で[[村社]]に列せられている。 == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[宝暦]]2年（[[1752年]]）再建。[[2014年]]（[[平成]]26年）10月に改修。 * 拝殿 - [[1988年]]（[[昭和]]63年）再建。天井画は[[文化勲章]]を受章した画家・[[山口華楊]]の実兄である[[山口玲熙]]（やまぐちれいき）によるもの。 * 社務所 * 参集殿 * [[富岡鉄斎]]の筆塚 - 当社の元宮司で近代日本画の巨匠・富岡鉄斎が生前に用いた筆2,000本以上が納められている。 * 神門 * 社号柱 - 社号は富岡鉄斎の筆。 == 境内社 == * 芸能神社（げいのうじんじゃ） - 祭神：[[アメノウズメ|天宇受売命]]。[[1957年]]（昭和32年）創建。[[芸能]]・[[芸術]]の分野で活動する人々より崇敬を受けている。芸能人やその関係者によって奉納された約4,000枚もの朱塗りの玉垣が社殿を囲む。 * 八百万神社 - 祭神：八百万の神々 * [[稲荷神社]] - 祭神：[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂神]]。辰巳稲荷神社とも呼ばれる。 * 水神社 - 祭神：龍神 * 清めの社 * [[清少納言]]社 - 祭神：清少納言 * 弁天神社（滄海神社） - 祭神：[[弁財天]]（[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]） * [[大国主]]神社 - 祭神：大国主大神（[[大黒天]]） Kurumazaki Shrine140517NI1.JPG|表参道 Kurumazaki-jinja, ura-sandō.jpg|裏参道 Kurumazaki Shrine140517NI4.JPG|四の鳥居とかつての中門 Kurumazaki-jinja, 2-no-torii 01.jpg|以前の二の鳥居 Kurumazaki Shrine140517NI5.JPG|拝殿 Kurumazaki Shrine140517NI6.JPG|500円で販売されている祈念神石 ファイル:Ōkuninushi-jinja inKurumazaki-jinja, Kyōto.jpg|大国主神社 ファイル:Kurumazaki Shrine140517NI3.JPG|芸能神社 ファイル:折獄龜鑑補(五).djvu|折獄龜鑑補 == 祭事 == [[例祭]]は5月14日で、その延長神事として5月第3日曜日に「三船祭」が行われる。[[昭和天皇]]即位を記念して1928年（昭和3年）より催行されているもので、[[嵐山]]の[[桂川 (淀川水系)|大堰川]]にて平安時代の船遊びが再現される。 == 文化財 == === 重要美術品 === * [[三略]] - [[清原頼業]]の筆によると伝えられる「三略」が[[重要美術品]]に認定されている。 === その他 === 画家・[[富岡鉄斎]]が一時当社の[[宮司]]を務めていた関係から、鉄斎の作品が多数伝わっており、「'''車軒文庫'''」として管理されている。また、社名標や本殿の扁額は鉄斎の筆によるものである。 == 時代劇ロケ == 車折神社は、東映や松竹の撮影所が近くにあることから、映画やドラマのロケによく使用されている。以下は、車折神社で撮影を行った主な時代劇のタイトル。 * [[暴れん坊将軍|吉宗評判記 暴れん坊将軍]] * [[松平右近事件帳]] * [[長七郎江戸日記]] * [[名奉行 遠山の金さん]] * [[半七捕物帳]] * [[忠臣蔵]] * [[はんなり菊太郎]] * [[水戸黄門]] * [[徳川風雲録]] * [[密命 寒月霞斬り]] == 前後の札所 == ; [[神仏霊場巡拝の道]] : 90 [[神護寺]]　-　'''91 車折神社'''　-　92 [[仁和寺]] == 交通 == * [[京福電気鉄道|京福電鉄]][[京福電気鉄道嵐山本線|嵐山本線]]（嵐電）「[[車折神社駅]]」下車すぐ南側 * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]]（[[山陰本線]]）「[[嵯峨嵐山駅]]」下車 徒歩約10分 * [[阪急電鉄]][[阪急嵐山線|嵐山線]]「[[嵐山駅 (阪急)|嵐山駅]]」下車 徒歩約15分 * [[京都市営バス]]「車折神社前」下車 徒歩3分 * [[京都バス]]「車折神社前（嵯峨美術大学・[[嵯峨美術短期大学]]前）」下車 徒歩約3分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp 車折神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{pref-stub|pref=京都府}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くるまさきしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:右京区の歴史]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:臨済宗天龍寺派の寺院]] [[Category:神仏霊場巡拝の道]] [[Category:清原氏 (広澄流)|社くるまさきしんしや]] [[Category:右京区の建築物]] [[Category:嵯峨野・嵐山]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kurumazaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecration_year = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine su11enly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the Rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] * '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}} {{citation needed|Kurumazaki Shrine}}
{{translated page|ja|車折神社|version=104355782|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kurumazaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecration_year = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine su11enly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the Rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] * '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}}
{{translated page|ja|車折神社|version=104355782|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kurumazaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecration_year = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine su11enly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the Rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] * '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}}

=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] * '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}}

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kurumazaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecration_year = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine su11enly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the Rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] * '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}}

=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] * '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]

'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine su11enly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the Rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] * '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.

'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine su11enly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the Rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] * '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}}
{{translated page|ja|車折神社|version=104355782|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] * '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}}

{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kurumazaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecration_year = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[:en:1189|1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[:en:1189|1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine su11enly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the Rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[:en:1752|1752]]), renovated in [[2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] * '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}}

The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine su11enly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the Rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[:en:1752|1752]]), renovated in [[:en:2014|2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[:en:1988|1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[:en:Abarenbō Shōgun|Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] * '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.

'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[:en:1189|1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine su11enly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the Rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[:en:1752|1752]]), renovated in [[:en:2014|2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[:en:1988|1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[:en:Abarenbō Shōgun|Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] * '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.

{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kurumazaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecration_year = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[:en:1189|1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[:en:1189|1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine su11enly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the Rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[:en:1752|1752]]), renovated in [[:en:2014|2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[:en:1988|1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[:en:Arashiyama|Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[:en:Abarenbō Shōgun|Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] * '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[:en:Arashiyama Main Line|Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[:en:Arashiyama Line|Arashiyama Line]]'s [[Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}}
{{translated page|ja|車折神社|version=104355782|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[:en:Arashiyama|Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[:en:Abarenbō Shōgun|Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] * '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[:en:Arashiyama Main Line|Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[:en:Arashiyama Line|Arashiyama Line]]'s [[:en:Arashiyama Station|Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}}

{{Otheruses|Kurumazaki Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City|Kurumazaki Shrine in Kakamigahara City, Gifu Prefecture|Kurumazaki Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kurumazaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:車折神社.jpg|270px|Main hall of Kurumazaki Shrine]] |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], Saga Asahicho 23 |ISO = JP-26 |Latitude = 35.0092 |Longitude = 135.6892 |Deities = [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] ([[Kurumazaki Myōjin]]) |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Village shrine|Murasha]] |consecration_year = [[Bunji]] 5 ([[:en:1189|1189]]) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alias = Hōjuin (寶壽院), Kurumazaki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] #91 (Kyoto #11) |Annual festival = [[May 14]] |Rituals = [[Mifune Matsuri]] |Map = {{location map|Kyoto city|lat=35.0092|long=135.6892|label=Kurumazaki Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kurumazaki Shrine''' (車折神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Saga Asahicho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It was previously classified as a [[village shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]] and is now an independent shrine not affiliated with the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is also written as "車柝神社"<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=嵯峨名所 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1082977 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2022-09-24 |language=ja}}</ref>.
== deity == The enshrined deity is [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]] (清原頼業), a renowned scholar of [[:en:Chinese classics|Chinese classics]] and [[Confucianism]] during the [[Heian period]]. A member of the prominent [[Kiyohara clan (Hirosumi lineage)|Hirosumi lineage]] of the [[Kiyohara clan]], Yoriyuki held the office of [[Ōgō no Daigeki|Ōgō]] for many years and was highly regarded by [[Fujiwara no Kanezane]], who described his talents as "worthy of reverence, like those of a god."
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the tomb of [[Kiyohara no Yoriyuki]], established on Kiyohara clan land in [[Bunji]] 5 ([[:en:1189|1189]]) after his death. Later, a temple named Hōjuin was built in his honor, and the shrine was established within the temple grounds. The site became known as the "Shrine of the Cherry Blossoms" (桜の宮) due to Yoriyuki's love of [[:en:Cherry blossoms|Cherry blossoms]] and the many trees planted in the area.
The name "Kurumazaki" (車折) has two possible origins: 1. A cart passing in front of the shrine su11enly broke apart. 2. During [[Emperor Go-Saga]]'s boating excursion on the [[Oigawa River (Kyoto)|Ōi River]], the cart carrying the Emperor mysteriously stopped at the shrine. When asked, locals explained that Yoriyuki was enshrined there. The Emperor later bestowed the title "Kurumazaki Daimyōjin" and the Rank of [[Shōichi-i]] (正一位).
During the [[Muromachi period]], Hōjuin became a sub-temple of [[Tenryu-ji]]. Widespread reverence for the shrine began during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
After the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] of the [[Meiji era]], Kurumazaki Shrine became independent but fell into disrepair. It was restored under the leadership of [[Tomioka Tessai]], a renowned painter and scholar who served as its priest.
== Features == * Main Hall - Reconstructed in [[Hōreki]] 2 ([[:en:1752|1752]]), renovated in [[:en:2014|2014]]. * Worship Hall - Built in [[:en:1988|1988]], featuring ceiling paintings by [[Yamaguchi Reiki]], brother of artist [[Yamaguchi Kayo]]. * Office, assembly hall, and [[Tomioka Tessai]]'s "Pen Mound" housing over 2,000 of his brushes. * Shrine gate and name plaques inscribed by [[Tomioka Tessai]].
=== Sub-Shrines === * **Entertainment Shrine** (芸能神社) - Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], attracting worshippers from the arts and entertainment industry. * **Yaoyorozu Shrine** - Dedicated to the Eight Million Gods. * **Inari Shrine** - Known as Tatsumi Inari Shrine, enshrining [[Uka no Mitama]]. * **Water Shrine** - Enshrining a dragon god. * **Sei Shōnagon Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Sei Shōnagon]]. * **Benten Shrine** (Sōkai Shrine) - Dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** - Dedicated to [[Ōkuninushi]].
== Annual Events == The shrine’s annual festival takes place on May 14, followed by the "Mifune Matsuri" on the third Sunday of May, commemorating the [[Shōwa Emperor]]'s enthronement. The festival recreates Heian-era boat festivities on the [[Oigawa River]] in [[:en:Arashiyama|Arashiyama]].
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * **"Sanryaku"** - A text attributed to Kiyohara no Yoriyuki.
=== Other Cultural Highlights === * Artifacts and works by [[Tomioka Tessai]], including his inscriptions on the shrine’s name plaques and main hall.
== Filming location == Due to its proximity to [[Toei]] and [[Shochiku]] studios, Kurumazaki Shrine frequently serves as a filming location for historical dramas, such as: * [[:en:Abarenbō Shōgun|Abarenbō Shōgun]] * [[Mito Kōmon]] * [[Chūshingura]]
== Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism]] : 90 [[Jingo-ji]] * '''91 Kurumazaki Shrine''' - 92 [[Ninna-ji]]
== Access == * Adjacent to [[Keifuku Electric Railroad|Keifuku Railway]]'s [[:en:Arashiyama Main Line|Arashiyama Main Line]] ("Randen") at [[Kurumazaki Shrine Station]]. * 10-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Sagano Line]]'s [[Saga-Arashiyama Station]]. * 15-minute walk from [[Hankyu Railway]]'s [[:en:Arashiyama Line|Arashiyama Line]]'s [[:en:Arashiyama Station|Arashiyama Station]]. * 3-minute walk from [[Kyoto City Bus]] or [[Kyoto Bus]] stops near Kurumazaki Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kurumazaki-jinja}} * [http://www.kurumazakijinja.or.jp Kurumazaki Shrine Official Site] {{Shinto}} {{stub|Kyoto Prefecture|Japanese history|Shinto|Buddhism}}
